description: Add concurrency safety: file locking, atomic writes for state.yaml, lock_id for runner exclusivity, advisory locks for JSONL appends.
steps: Create kernel/file_lock.py with: (a) class FileLock that uses a .lock file with PID and timestamp, (b) acquire(timeout=10) method that creates lock file atomically (open with O_CREAT|O_EXCL), (c) release() method that removes lock file, (d) is_stale(max_age_seconds=600) classmethod that checks if lock is older than max_age, (e) context manager support (__enter__/__exit__), (f) force_release() classmethod for --force override of stale locks
Create kernel/atomic_write.py with: atomic_write(path, content) function that writes to path.tmp then os.rename() for POSIX atomicity
Modify memory/state_manager.py save_state() to use atomic_write instead of direct file write

Add lock_id field to state: when runner starts, generate a UUID lock_id and write it. On startup, check if lock_id exists and last_updated is < 10 minutes ago. If so, refuse to start (raise RuntimeError with clear message). Add --force support that clears stale locks.
Add advisory locking wrapper for JSONL append operations in state_manager (record_decision, record_reflection) using FileLock
Create tests/test_file_lock.py with tests: basic acquire/release, stale lock detection, force release, context manager, concurrent simulation (threading), atomic write correctness
Run tests to confirm everything passes
acceptance_criteria: kernel/file_lock.py exists with FileLock class supporting acquire/release/context manager
Lock staleness detection works (locks older than 10 min are considered stale)
JSONL append operations use advisory locking
All new tests pass with coverage >= 90%
findings: All 28 new tests pass. Full suite 741 tests pass with 98.87% coverage. Added FileLock class with O_CREAT|O_EXCL atomic acquisition, stale detection, force release. Added atomic_write with temp-then-rename. StateManager.save_state uses atomic_write. record_decision and record_reflection use advisory FileLock. Added runner lock management (acquire/check/release). FileLock constructor accepts timeout parameter used by context manager.
